activities.markdown:
# Reading Drum Notation

Drum notation uses a standard 5-line staff. Each line/space = a different drum:

| Symbol | Drum/Cymbal |
|--------|-------------|
| × on top space | Hi-hat |
| × above staff | Open hi-hat |
| × on 3rd space | Ride cymbal |
| × above top line | Crash cymbal |
| ○ on 3rd line | Snare drum |
| ○ on 2nd space | Hi tom |
| ○ on 1st space | Floor tom |
| ○ below staff | Bass drum |

X-shaped noteheads = cymbals. Round noteheads = drums.

activities.question: What does an X-shaped notehead represent in drum notation?
activities.explanation: X-shaped noteheads (×) represent cymbals (hi-hat, crash, ride). Round noteheads (○) represent drums (snare, kick, toms).
